headline: Chromosome model links one steady motor to shape shift needed for cell division
summary: It's tricky to make an exact copy of yourself. Rice University professor Peter Wolynes is interested in how the packaged DNA, called a chromosome, changes its structure during replication, going from a ball shape to a cylinder shape that can be transported easily to the daughter cell.
